description_long: This object consists of two large, medieval-style swords with broad, straight blades and pointed tips. The larger sword features a wide, flat blade with a dark, mottled metallic finish, a long crossguard, and a simple cylindrical handle ending in a rounded pommel. The smaller sword has a narrower blade, also straight and pointed, with a slightly ornate crossguard and a shorter handle. Both swords exhibit a weathered, battle-worn appearance, with visible texture variations on the blade surfaces. The crossguards and handles are dark, likely made of metal, and the overall design is utilitarian with minimal decoration. These swords would be imposing and functional in an urban fantasy or historical context.
description: Two large, straight-bladed swords with dark, weathered metal surfaces and simple crossguards, one broad and one narrow.
description_view_0: Front view showing both swords side by side, highlighting the broadness of the larger blade and the relative slenderness of the smaller one.
description_view_1: Edge-on view showing the thin profile of both blades and the crossguard of the smaller sword.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the backs of both swords and the symmetry of their blades and handles.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, again emphasizing the thinness and straightness of the blades.
attribute_handle_design: straight, cylindrical, dark metal with simple rounded pommel
